COUNCIL BUSINESS (A) Presentation: Horizon Council Foundation (Councilmember Wilson) Glen Salyer, Economic Development Director of Lee County and Assistant to County Manager, through a PowerPoint presentation, provided information regarding economic development partnership; Horizon Council; strategic support; commercial corridors; task forces; Horizon Foundation; industrial development authority; economic development office; business development; partnership successes; and success stories. Councilmember Wilson moved approval of the first reading of Ordinance No. 2015-20 and that the ordinance be moved to second reading, seconded by Councilmember Ribble, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. (C) _ First Reading and Public Hearing: Ordinance No. 2015-19 An Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida, Amending Chapter 34, Zoning, Article VII, Supplementary District Regulations, Division 15, Estero Land Development Code, to Limit Certain Excavation Activities Relating to Well Stimulation Including Acid Stimulation and Hydraulic or Acid Fracturing; Providing for Codification; Providing for Conflict with Other Ordinances; and Providing for an Effective Date The title of Ordinance No. 2015-19 was read by Village Clerk Hall. Mayor Batos opened the public hearing and called for public testimony. Public Testimony: Phil Gresh, Shadow Wood resident, stated he was against the Bill introduced by Representative Rodrigues and addressed concern regarding fracking. (Provided written materials) Ray Judah, Florida Coastal and Ocean Coalition, encouraged the Council to move forward with the ordinance and resolution. Larry Newell, Fountain Lakes resident, stated that this needed to be carried on to Congress in firm terms. Noah Kugler, Professional Geologist, representing Conservancy of Southwest Florida, spoke to concern regarding fracking and responded to questions from Councilmembers. Peter Cangialosi, noted that the Council was not alone in what they were doing regarding fracking and read from information that he provided from the Tallahassee Democrat. (Provided written materials) Ralf Brookes, Attorney, Conservancy of Southwest Florida, stated that they supported the ordinance; fracking was not appropriate for Estero; and addressed Home Rule power. He also responded to questions from the Council. (Provided written materials) Jennifer Hecker, Director of Natural Resource Policy, Conservancy of Southwest Florida, conveyed appreciation that the Council was going forward with the ordinance; addressed concer regarding recent House Bills; and responded to questions from the Council. Phil Douglas, Lighthouse Bay resident, stated that Representative Rodrigues was representing the petroleum business; the Council was representing Estero residents. He believed that the ordinance was a powerful message. Donna Knapp, Pine Ridge resident, conveyed appreciation to the Council. Julie Gerhard (no address given) conveyed appreciation to the Council for supporting the ordinance. No further public testimony; the hearing was closed. (D) (E) Council discussion ensued. Councilmember Ribble moved to approve first reading of Ordinance No. 2015-19 and that the ordinance be moved to second reading, seconded by Councilmember Errington, called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote. project_title: passed the ordinance and the Bill passed as written, the ordinance would be preempted; how
summary: passed the ordinance and the Bill passed as written, the ordinance would be preempted; however, if the wording in the Bill was rewritten, the portion that would preempt the Village from controlling the zoning would be reinstated. He continued that he was not in favor of fracking; however, he wanted to be practical and put in laws that meant something. He stated that his responsibility was to represent the people; although he did not believe that the ordinance would accomplish anything, he would be in favor of the ordinance because he sensed that was what most people wanted. The motion was called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote, with Councilmember Brown absent. The meeting went into recess at 7:55 p.m. and reconvened at 8:02 p.m. First Reading: Ordinance No. 2015-21 A Zoning Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida; Rezoning 21.36 Acres Located at 9201 and 9301 Corkscrew Road, from Commercial Planned Development to Residential Planned Development for the Volunteers of America Continuing Care/Assisted Living Project, with a Maximum 340 Beds and Maximum Building Height of 45 Feet, and Including Conditions of Development; Approving Certain Deviations; Providing for Conflict; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance 2015-21and noted that the zoning hearing would be held on January 13, 2015 and the public hearing notice was published pursuant to Florida statutes, on December 11, 2015. Community Development Director Gibbs provided a brief explanation of the ordinance as well as information related to the zoning hearing process. Note
outcome: passed the ordinance and the Bill passed as written, the ordinance would be preempted; however, if the wording in the Bill was rewritten, the portion that would preempt the Village from controlling the zoning would be reinstated. He continued that he was not in favor of fracking; however, he wanted to be practical and put in laws that meant something. He stated that his responsibility was to represent the people; although he did not believe that the ordinance would accomplish anything, he would be in favor of the ordinance because he sensed that was what most people wanted. The motion was called and carried unanimously through a roll call vote, with Councilmember Brown absent. The meeting went into recess at 7:55 p.m. and reconvened at 8:02 p.m. First Reading: Ordinance No. 2015-21 A Zoning Ordinance of the Village Council of the Village of Estero, Florida; Rezoning 21.36 Acres Located at 9201 and 9301 Corkscrew Road, from Commercial Planned Development to Residential Planned Development for the Volunteers of America Continuing Care/Assisted Living Project, with a Maximum 340 Beds and Maximum Building Height of 45 Feet, and Including Conditions of Development; Approving Certain Deviations; Providing for Conflict; Providing for Severability; and Providing an Effective Date Village Clerk Hall read the title of Ordinance 2015-21and noted that the zoning hearing would be held on January 13, 2015 and the public hearing notice was published pursuant to Florida statutes, on December 11, 2015. project_title: Approved Ordinance No. 2019-29 with revisions: (1) Condition 8 ­ add the following languag
summary: Approved Ordinance No. 2019-29 with revisions: (1) Condition 8 ­ add the following language: "Staff will review and approve, at the time of Development Order, a plan for management of construction traffic including but not limited to posting of signs and other methods ofcommunication." (2) Condition 19, subparagraph b., second sentence to read: "Main Paseo walkways will be raised and constructed ofpaver bricks or similar materials." (3) Add a new condition: "33. Public Access Easement. Consistent with the Village Comprehensive Plan, placemaking elements including the pedestrian plaza roads and reverse frontage road, public park, promenade and public open space, and sidewalks shall be accessible to the public through dedication of a public access easement in favor of the Village of Estero and acceptable to the Village Attorney." (4) Deviation 12 new wording- "Deviation 12 (Connection separation for the mixed use parcel) Deviation from LDC Section 10-285(a) [Table 1] which requires a minimum roadway connection for separation distance of60' for access roads or accessways to allow a minimum separation of 48' for the mixed use parcel access from the internal accessway serving the multi-family portion ofthe project."
outcome: Approved Ordinance No. 2019-29 with revisions: (1) Condition 8 ­ add the following language: "Staff will review and approve, at the time of Development Order, a plan for management of construction traffic including but not limited to posting of signs and other methods ofcommunication." (2) Condition 19, subparagraph b., second sentence to read: "Main Paseo walkways will be raised and constructed ofpaver bricks or similar materials." (3) Add a new condition: "33. Public Access Easement. Consistent with the Village Comprehensive Plan, placemaking elements including the pedestrian plaza roads and reverse frontage road, public park, promenade and public open space, and sidewalks shall be accessible to the public through dedication of a public access easement in favor of the Village of Estero and acceptable to the Village Attorney." (4) Deviation 12 new wording- "Deviation 12 (Connection separation for the mixed use parcel) Deviation from LDC Section 10-285(a) [Table 1] which requires a minimum roadway connection for separation distance of60' for access roads or accessways to allow a minimum separation of 48' for the mixed use parcel access from the internal accessway serving the multi-family portion ofthe project."

project_title: APPROVED BY THE COUNCIL MAY 27, 2020 COVID-19 NOTICE: Pursuant to Executive Order No. 20-6
summary: APPROVED BY THE COUNCIL MAY 27, 2020 COVID-19 NOTICE: Pursuant to Executive Order No. 20-69, issued by the Office of Governor Ron DeSantis on March 20, 2020, municipalities may conduct meetings of their governing boards without having a quorum ofits members present physically or at any specific location, and utilizing communications media technology such as telephonic or video conferencing, as provided by Section 120.54(5)(b )2, Florida Statutes. The Village ofEstero held this virtual public meeting utilizing the Zoom Meetings app, allowing Councilmembers and Village staff to attend the public meeting remotely, along with broadcasting live via the Village of Estero website link: https://cstcro­ fl.gov/council/watch-mectings-online/ FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES VILLAGE COUNCIL WORKSHOP Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 April 22, 2020 9:30 a.m. 1. CALL TO ORDER: 9:30 a.m. INVOCATION: Offered by Vice Mayor Errington. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Led by Kyle Coleman, Assistant to Village Manager. ROLL CALL: Present: Mayor Bill Ribble - District I, Vice Mayor Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant Village Attorney Derek Rooney, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Public Works Director David Willems, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. Village Council Workshop Minutes April 22, 2020 Page 1 of3 2. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero Staff Presentation/Comments: Village Manager Sarkozy. Presentation/Comments: Chris Patricca, District 3 representative on the School District ofLee County School Board. The purpose ofthe discussion was to determine whether there was consensus among Councilmembers on where the School District should locate their two new facilities. Options considered were: (1) locating adjacent to the Estero High School site on Williams Road; and, (2) building on a Three Oaks Parkway 70-acre site. Council Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch, Batos, Levitan, Wilson, McLain, Vice Mayor Errington and Mayor Ribble. Consensus ofthe Council indicated four in favor ofthe Three Oaks Parkway site and three in favor ofthe Williams Road location
outcome: APPROVED BY THE COUNCIL MAY 27, 2020 COVID-19 NOTICE: Pursuant to Executive Order No. 20-69, issued by the Office of Governor Ron DeSantis on March 20, 2020, municipalities may conduct meetings of their governing boards without having a quorum ofits members present physically or at any specific location, and utilizing communications media technology such as telephonic or video conferencing, as provided by Section 120.54(5)(b )2, Florida Statutes. The Village ofEstero held this virtual public meeting utilizing the Zoom Meetings app, allowing Councilmembers and Village staff to attend the public meeting remotely, along with broadcasting live via the Village of Estero website link: https://cstcro­ fl.gov/council/watch-mectings-online/ FINAL ACTION AGENDA/MINUTES VILLAGE COUNCIL WORKSHOP Village of Estero 9401 Corkscrew Palms Circle Estero, FL 33928 April 22, 2020 9:30 a.m. 1. CALL TO ORDER: 9:30 a.m. INVOCATION: Offered by Vice Mayor Errington. PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE: Led by Kyle Coleman, Assistant to Village Manager. ROLL CALL: Present: Mayor Bill Ribble - District I, Vice Mayor Katy Errington - District 4, Councilmember Howard Levitan - District 2, Councilmember Jon McLain - District 3, Councilmember Jim Boesch - District 5, Councilmember Nick Batos - District 6, and Councilmember Jim Wilson - District 7. Also present: Village Manager Steve Sarkozy, Village Attorney Burt Saunders, Assistant Village Attorney Derek Rooney, Assistant to Village Manager Kyle Coleman, Public Works Director David Willems, Finance Director Lisa Roberson, Community Development Director Mary Gibbs, and Village Clerk Kathy Hall. Village Council Workshop Minutes April 22, 2020 Page 1 of3 2. WORKSHOP ITEMS: (a) Discussion: Location ofTwo New School Facilities in Estero Staff Presentation/Comments: Village Manager Sarkozy. Presentation/Comments: Chris Patricca, District 3 representative on the School District ofLee County School Board. The purpose ofthe discussion was to determine whether there was consensus among Councilmembers on where the School District should locate their two new facilities. Council Questions or Comments: Councilmembers Boesch, Batos, Levitan, Wilson, McLain, Vice Mayor Errington and Mayor Ribble.
